meta.description: All illegal transitions in the synthetic mini-language system. A transition is illegal if it has zero probability (structurally absent edge). Use this to validate generated sequences.
meta.note_on_verb_adj_categories: Verb-category and adjective-category transition matrices are not yet defined. Until they are, all intra-verb-category and intra-adjective-category transitions are structurally absent.

pos_illegal_transitions.description: Illegal transitions between POS types (START, NOUN, VERB, ADJ, END). Any sequence containing one of these consecutive POS pairs is illegal.

transitions.reason: Sentences cannot begin with a verb

Adjective must resolve to NOUN before a VERB can appear
No adjective chaining
Sentence cannot end on an adjective
START is a one-way entry boundary
No verb chaining
Sentence cannot end on a verb
START is a one-way entry boundary
END is absorbing — no exit
END is absorbing — no exit
END is absorbing — no exit
END is absorbing — no exit
END is absorbing — no exit

noun_category_illegal_transitions.description: Illegal transitions between noun subcategories. These apply whenever a NOUN->NOUN POS transition is taken.

noun_to_verb_illegal_transitions.description: Category-level illegal transitions from NOUN categories to VERB categories. If a pair is missing, it is allowed. These constraints define semantic incompatibility in the synthetic language environment.

transitions.reason: Inert objects cannot initiate physical actions in this model (no agency)
Objects cannot perform cognition
Objects lack social agency
Devices do not have cognition in this system
Devices are not social agents
Locations do not execute actions (they are contexts, not agents)
Locations do not possess cognition
Locations are not agents in social interaction
Abstract systems do not perform physical actions directly
Abstract entities are not social actors
